Towards Simplicity
Alexis Bourbeau
💎
Apr 21, 2026
1. No more Community Activity tab

As a first step towards simplifying our community interface, I've removed the "Community Activity" feed that used to be at the top of the list of spaces.

It was an aggregator which pulled in posts from any space, but the vast majority of you either didn't make use of it or found it confusing, so that's why it's no longer there.

For the folks that were using it, apologies! But now perhaps it'll be easier to just click/tap directly into the main Connect & Discuss section, which is a linear posting setup more like Skool/Facebook etc.

2. No more Live chat (for now)

The second step is deprecating the live chat for the time being. It was definitely pulling good questions away from the main Connect & Discuss feed (and the deep dive What Would You Do? as well). A lot of valuable questions/answers, shares, and wins were getting lost in the flow (which can't be searched or preserved).

There are some other potential "opt-in" solutions for people that really just want that quick chat experience, like a larger group DM or separate invite-only chat space. But I think it may make sense to let things settle into a more simple, old-skool experience to see how we all feel. We can always revisit in the near future.

3. Keep iterating based on the community's input

We may continue to compress things down to just a single feed in the future, or make additional changes based on what we hear about your experience. One thing is for sure though; we'll keep on trying to improve things!

Feedback?

I'm sure there will be a ton, since we're balancing a lot of different preferences here. You can comment here with any input or suggestions, or feel free to DM me directly.
Thanks for your patience and willingness to adapt – both necessary traits in vending and our community too!

March numbers are in-Finally hit $1M run rate!
Tim Barnes
Apr 4, 2026

Ben Miller and I just had a growth spurt and hit $98,009.21 in sales in March. This was our first month at a $1 million run rate—and ALMOST hit $100k monthly (darn that $1,990.79 lol). Quite a few of our new locations only had a partial month in March, so I'm hoping we'll hit $100k next month. Goal for end of 2026: 60 locations and $232,000 monthly run rate. Oh wow, that made me nervous just typing it, but big goals motivate us.

This group has been life-changing for us. I can't say enough that if you're willing to put in the work, all the tools and support you need for success are here. We joined in August 2024. Two months later we had our first machine (it was an awful location, but that's another story lol), 17 months later, we hit $98k in monthly sales.

My (unsolicited) advice: First, read EVERYTHING in the modules. Like Anthony Kolodziej told us, I spent the first few weeks of membership like it was night school. I read everything I could, watched every video, I made cheat sheets on every machine type, the pros/cons and specs. Don't expect anyone else to do the work for you. Second, don't give up. This wasn't easy—There are plenty of frustrations, challenges, and moments you think you can't do it. Keep going! Third, deliver. Our biggest priority is to work hard to deliver the best experience to every one of our locations. We've done everything from 1am stock runs at University housing with screaming crowds of college students all around us asking for free stuff, to patiently talking a girl off the ledge because our machine stole her $5 but didn't unlock the door. It's not easy, but I love making people happy, providing an awesome service, and eventually seeing the rewards roll in.

Thank you to Mike Hoffmann and Anthony Kolodziej for their leadership, and to every member in this group that has helped and encouraged us along the way. Cheers to our collective success in 2026!

Welcome to "What Would You Do?"
Modern Amenities
⭐️
Mar 1, 2026

This is where you can share the situations that might benefit from the community's collective wisdom.

We've learned that when you lay out the real context — what’s happening, what you’re weighing, what feels uncertain — it gives others something solid to respond to.
You don't have to write an epic, but do try to give all the info that might help people understand the situation up front. This will avoid unnecessary back and forth.

Here we use the “article” format – just hit Create → Article – so you can title it clearly and then tell the full story. You can also share photos or other media if you need to.

If it helps, include as many details as you can that may actually shape the decision. Of course, different situations call for different context.

Some examples:

Considering a new property? Share the type of location, estimated foot traffic, possible specific placements, and what else you know so far.

Struggling with pop-ins? Let us know your area, the kinds of locations you’re targeting, how you're positioning yourself, and where the conversations tends to stall.

Trying to get a response from corporate? Explain who you’ve contacted, what you’ve proposed, and where it’s stalled.

Trying to decide what rev share or structure to propose? Share the context of the opportunity, traffic or sales expectations, and any constraints that might affect the deal.

Thinking about moving into a warehouse? Share your current setup, growth stage, and what’s prompting the move.

Looking at buying a route? Outline the numbers, equipment condition, contracts, and your goals.

Again, you don’t need to include everything — just enough so others can see the full picture. Because it's true that vague questions tend to get vague responses.

No situation is too small if it matters to you. – because sometimes those little everyday decisions are the ones that end up shaping your business.

So if you've got something on your mind, you've come to the right place.
Let's dig in.

Apr 23, 2026

Hey everyone quick question for those in vending/micro markets:

Is there actually such thing as a free coffee service for residential properties?

For those who’ve dealt with this:

Have you actually seen a true “free” coffee setup?

How would you handle this with a property that’s expecting it to be free?

Appreciate any insight here.
